event: invasive ductal carcinoma of the right breast
recently traveled to the Southwest United States
no known occupational or environmental exposures
computed tomography (CT) showed right upper lobe mass
biopsy of the lung and liver revealed metastatic adenocarcinoma
no actionable mutations on standard tumor molecular profiling
palliative systemic therapy with carboplatin, pemetrexed, and pembrolizumab every 21 days
imaging after first two cycles of therapy showed stable disease
CT angiogram of the chest showed no pulmonary embolism
confluent regions of consolidation in the lungs bilaterally
admitted to the intensive care unit
lung protective ventilation utilizing low tidal volume strategy
thin secretions in the left lower lobe
bronchoalveolar lavage studies showed neutrophilic predominance
transthoracic echocardiogram showed mild symmetric left ventricular hypertrophy
no signs of increased filling pressures
lung biopsy deferred due to clinical instability
initiated intravenous methylprednisolone 2 mg/kg/day on day three
repeat chest CT imaging on day 12 showed improvement
marked improvement in severe interstitial abnormality
organizing phase of acute lung injury
transitioned to prednisone 1 mg/kg/day after two weeks of methylprednisolone
oxygen needs decreased to 1.5 L per minute via nasal cannula
re-staging CT of the torso six weeks after admission
remaining bilateral subpleural interstitial fibrotic abnormality
